description: Security hotfix for improvements phase — YouTube URL validation, email HTML sanitization, crypto/rand tokens, reload race condition, SSRF prevention

userStories.title: Validate YouTube URLs in youtube-adapter
userStories.description: As the system, I need YouTube URLs to be validated before passing to yt-dlp so that malicious URLs cannot exploit the system.
acceptanceCriteria: YouTube URL must match pattern: https://(www.)youtube.com/watch?v= or https://youtu.be/
URL length limited to 200 characters
Invalid URLs rejected at config load with clear error log
Invalid URLs rejected at runtime with error response (not passed to yt-dlp)
Add context.WithTimeout (30s) to resolveStreamURL() yt-dlp execution
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Restrict and sanitize email sending endpoint
userStories.description: As the system, I need the email sending endpoint to be restricted to internal services and sanitize HTML content so that it cannot be abused.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/send-email only accepts requests from internal Docker network (check source IP or use internal-only mux)
Email body is sanitized: strip script, iframe, on* event handlers, javascript: URIs
Allow safe HTML tags: p, a, h1-h6, br, strong, em
Add http.MaxBytesReader (1MB limit) on request body
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Internal IP check, HTML sanitization (allowlist), MaxBytesReader 1MB

userStories.title: Replace UUID with crypto/rand for invitation tokens
userStories.description: As the system, I need invitation tokens to be cryptographically secure so that they cannot be predicted or brute-forced.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace uuid.New().String() with crypto/rand + encoding/hex (32 bytes = 64 hex chars)
Token format: 64-character hex string
Existing invitation verification logic works with new token format (string comparison)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replaced uuid.New().String() with crypto/rand (32 bytes) + hex encoding = 64-char hex token

userStories.title: Fix cctv-adapter reload race condition
userStories.description: As the system, I need camera reload to use a single lock scope so that concurrent access doesn't cause inconsistent state.
acceptanceCriteria: Reload() function uses a single cm.mu.Lock() for the entire read-old + compute-diff + update-config operation
No gap between reading old state and writing new state
Existing streams for unchanged cameras are not interrupted
Removed cameras are stopped, new cameras are started
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Single lock scope for entire read-old + compute-diff + update-config operation; process kills and goroutine launches moved outside lock

userStories.title: Validate camera source URLs to prevent SSRF
userStories.description: As the system, I need camera source URLs to be validated so that internal network resources cannot be accessed via SSRF.
acceptanceCriteria: RTSP source URLs must start with rtsp:// or rtsps://
YouTube source URLs must match YouTube domain pattern
Reject URLs containing localhost, 127.0.0.1, 10.*, 172.16-31.*, 192.168.*, [::1]
Validation applied in POST /api/cameras and PUT /api/cameras/{id}
Invalid URLs return 400 with descriptive error message
Typecheck passes
